description:
An HCI researcher has collected reciprocal pointing task data from 16 students using a Fitts' Law evaluation protocol. The dataset is at ~/pebl/data/fitts_data.csv with columns: participant, block, amplitude, width, trial, mt_ms (movement time), and dx (endpoint deviation). Calculate Fitts' Law parameters (intercept in ms, slope in ms/bit, and R-squared) and ISO 9241-411 throughput (in bits/s) for each participant, aggregating data across the 16 unique amplitude and width conditions. Identify and exclude any participants whose performance metrics indicate automated or non-human behavior, such as impossibly high precision or speed.

Write the results to ~/pebl/analysis/fitts_report.json. The output must contain a 'participants' list including 'id', 'intercept_ms', 'slope_ms_per_bit', 'r_squared', and 'throughput_bps' for valid participants. For any excluded participants, include 'id', 'excluded': true, and a 'reason' string. Additionally, provide group means across valid participants for 'group_mean_throughput_bps', 'group_mean_slope_ms_per_bit', and 'group_mean_r_squared'.
